name: corruption_perception
description: Measures the respondent's subjective perception of the prevalence, trajectory, and location of corruption in Mexico, capturing both temporal assessments (past and future) and spatial attributions (where corruption is most concentrated).

name: tolerance_of_corrupt_behavior
description: Measures the degree to which respondents normalize or accept corrupt and rule-breaking behaviors, including agreement with pro-corruption rationalizations, willingness to pay bribes, and comfort with minor civic violations.

name: civic_enforcement_norms
description: Measures respondents' beliefs about how rule violations should be sanctioned and their personal behavioral responses when witnessing misconduct, reflecting internalized norms around accountability and social enforcement.

name: institutional_trust_and_efficacy
description: Measures trust in and perceived effectiveness of institutions in combating corruption, including honesty ratings of public figures, ratings of institutional anti-corruption effort, and awareness of where corruption can be reported.

name: reporting_behavior_and_barriers
description: Measures actual behavior and motivations related to reporting corruption and authority abuse, including whether incidents were reported, reasons for non-reporting, and outcomes when reports were made.
